  20:   normalizeDataLayerSearchQuery,
  21:   toEffectiveRetrievalLimit,
  22: } from './domain.js'
  23: 
  24: export interface DataLayerAPI {
  25:   search(query: DataLayerSearchQuery): Promise<DataLayerSearchResponse>
  26:   getSession(id: string): Promise<SessionDetail | null>
  27:   listSessions(filter?: DataLayerListSessionsFilter): Promise<DataLayerListSessionsPage>
  28:   getCommitContext(sessionId: string): Promise<CommitContext | null>
  29:   getEntityGraph(entityName: string): Promise<EntityGraphView>
  30: }
  31: 
  32: export interface DataLayerDependencies {
  33:   retrieval: RetrievalTools
  34:   getSession(id: string): Promise<SessionDetail | null>
  35:   listSessions(filter?: DataLayerListSessionsFilter): Promise<DataLayerListSessionsPage>
  36:   getCommitContext(sessionId: string): Promise<CommitContext | null>
  37:   getEntityGraph(entityName: string): Promise<EntityGraphView>
  38:   getSessionForRef?(ref: RetrievalReference): Promise<SessionDetail | null>
  39:   getMemoryUnit?(id: string): Promise<MemoryUnit | null>
  40:   getSourceAsset?(id: string): Promise<SourceAsset | null>
  41:   getArchiveAnchor?(id: string): Promise<ArchiveAnchor | null>
  42: }
  43: 
  44: function mixedCandidateLimit(limit?: number): number {
  45:   const effectiveLimit = toEffectiveRetrievalLimit(limit)
  46:   if (effectiveLimit === Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) return effectiveLimit
  47:   return Math.min(Math.max(effectiveLimit * 4, effectiveLimit), 200)
  48: }
  49: 
  50: async function runSearch(
  51:   retrieval: RetrievalTools,
  52:   query: DataLayerSearchQuery,
  53: ): Promise<RetrievalReference[]> {
  54:   const executionQuery = query.limit === 0
  55:     ? { ...query, limit: toEffectiveRetrievalLimit(query.limit) }
  56:     : query
  57: 
  58:   switch (query.target) {
  59:     case 'session':
  60:       return retrieval.searchSessions(executionQuery)
  61:     case 'memory_unit':
  62:       return retrieval.searchMemoryUnits(executionQuery)
  63:     case 'source_asset':
  64:       return retrieval.searchSourceAssets ? retrieval.searchSourceAssets(executionQuery) : []
  65:     case 'archive_anchor':
  66:       return retrieval.searchArchiveAnchors ? retrieval.searchArchiveAnchors(executionQuery) : []
  67:     case 'mixed': {
  68:       const effectiveLimit = toEffectiveRetrievalLimit(query.limit)
  69:       const candidateQuery = {
  70:         ...executionQuery,
  71:         limit: mixedCandidateLimit(query.limit),
  72:       }
  73:       const results = await Promise.all([
  74:         retrieval.searchSessions(candidateQuery),
  75:         retrieval.searchMemoryUnits(candidateQuery),
  76:         retrieval.searchSourceAssets ? retrieval.searchSourceAssets(candidateQuery) : Promise.resolve([]),
  77:         retrieval.searchArchiveAnchors ? retrieval.searchArchiveAnchors(candidateQuery) : Promise.resolve([]),
  78:       ])
  79:       return dedupeRetrievalReferences(results.flat())
  80:         .sort(compareRetrievalReferences)
  81:         .slice(0, mixedCandidateLimit(effectiveLimit))
  82:     }
  83:   }
  84: 
  85:   return []
  86: }
  87: 
  88: async function hydrateSearchHit(
  89:   deps: DataLayerDependencies,
  90:   ref: RetrievalReference,
  91: ): Promise<DataLayerSearchHit> {
  92:   switch (ref.kind) {
  93:     case 'session': {
  94:       const session = await deps.getSession(ref.id)
  95:       return session ? { ref, session } : { ref }
  96:     }
  97:     case 'memory_unit': {
  98:       const memoryUnit = deps.getMemoryUnit ? await deps.getMemoryUnit(ref.id) : null
  99:       const session = deps.getSessionForRef ? await deps.getSessionForRef(ref) : null
 100:       return {
 101:         ref,
 102:         ...(memoryUnit ? { memory_unit: memoryUnit } : {}),
 103:         ...(session ? { session } : {}),
 104:       }
 105:     }
 106:     case 'source_asset': {
 107:       const sourceAsset = deps.getSourceAsset
 108:         ? await deps.getSourceAsset(ref.id)
 109:         : await deps.retrieval.getSourceAsset(ref.id)
 110:       const session = deps.getSessionForRef ? await deps.getSessionForRef(ref) : null
 111:       return {
 112:         ref,
 113:         ...(sourceAsset ? { source_asset: sourceAsset } : {}),
 114:         ...(session ? { session } : {}),
 115:       }
 116:     }
 117:     case 'archive_anchor': {
 118:       const archiveAnchor = deps.getArchiveAnchor
 119:         ? await deps.getArchiveAnchor(ref.id)
 120:         : await deps.retrieval.getArchiveAnchor(ref.id)
 121:       const session = deps.getSessionForRef ? await deps.getSessionForRef(ref) : null
 122:       return {
 123:         ref,
 124:         ...(archiveAnchor ? { archive_anchor: archiveAnchor } : {}),
 125:         ...(session ? { session } : {}),
 126:       }
 127:     }
 128:     case 'commit':
 129:       return { ref }
 130:   }
 131: }
 132: 
 133: function mixedHitPriority(hit: DataLayerSearchHit): number {
 134:   switch (hit.ref.kind) {
 135:     case 'memory_unit':
 136:       return 0
 137:     case 'source_asset':
 138:       return 1
 139:     case 'archive_anchor':
 140:       return 2
 141:     case 'session':
 142:       return 3
 143:     case 'commit':
 144:       return 4
 145:   }
 146: }
 147: 
 148: function compareMixedHits(left: DataLayerSearchHit, right: DataLayerSearchHit): number {
 149:   return mixedHitPriority(left) - mixedHitPriority(right)
 150:     || compareRetrievalReferences(left.ref, right.ref)
 151: }
 152: 
 153: function normalizeMixedHits(
 154:   hits: DataLayerSearchHit[],
 155:   limit?: number,
 156: ): DataLayerSearchHit[] {
 157:   const coveredSessions = new Set(
 158:     hits
 159:       .filter((hit) => hit.ref.kind !== 'session' && typeof hit.session?.session_id === 'string')
 160:       .map((hit) => hit.session!.session_id),
 161:   )
 162: 
 163:   return hits
 164:     .filter((hit) => !(hit.ref.kind === 'session'
 165:       && typeof hit.session?.session_id === 'string'
 166:       && coveredSessions.has(hit.session.session_id)))
 167:     .sort(compareMixedHits)
 168:     .slice(0, toEffectiveRetrievalLimit(limit))
 169: }
 170: 
 171: export function createDataLayerAPI(deps: DataLayerDependencies): DataLayerAPI {
 172:   return {
 173:     async search(query: DataLayerSearchQuery): Promise<DataLayerSearchResponse> {
 174:       const normalized = normalizeDataLayerSearchQuery(query)
 175:       const refs = await runSearch(deps.retrieval, normalized)
 176:       const hits = await Promise.all(refs.map((ref) => hydrateSearchHit(deps, ref)))
 177:       return {
 178:         query: normalized,
 179:         hits: normalized.target === 'mixed'
 180:           ? normalizeMixedHits(hits, normalized.limit)
 181:           : hits,
 182:       }
 183:     },
 184: 
 185:     getSession(id: string): Promise<SessionDetail | null> {
 186:       return deps.getSession(id)
 187:     },
 188: 
 189:     listSessions(filter?: DataLayerListSessionsFilter): Promise<DataLayerListSessionsPage> {
 190:       return deps.listSessions(filter)
 191:     },
 192: 
 193:     getCommitContext(sessionId: string): Promise<CommitContext | null> {
 194:       return deps.getCommitContext(sessionId)
 195:     },
 196: 
 197:     getEntityGraph(entityName: string): Promise<EntityGraphView> {
 198:       return deps.getEntityGraph(entityName)
 199:     },
 200:   }
 201: }

  13: } from '../storage/graph/graph-adapter.js'

  17: export const TRANSCRIPT_MEMORY_EXTRACTION_VERSION = 'transcript-memory-bundle.v1'
  18: export const TRANSCRIPT_MEMORY_EXTRACTOR = 'deterministic-transcript-baseline'
  19: 
  20: export interface MemoryGraphNode {
  21:   id: string
  22:   labels: string[]
  23:   properties: Record<string, unknown>
  24: }
  25: 
  26: export interface MemoryGraphEdge {
  27:   from_id: string
  28:   to_id: string
  29:   type: string
  30:   properties?: Record<string, unknown>
  31: }
  32: 
  33: export interface MemoryExtractionBundle {
  34:   extraction_version: string
  35:   extractor: string
  36:   generated_at: string
  37:   project: string
  38:   session_id: string
  39:   source_asset: SourceAsset
  40:   memory_units: MemoryUnit[]
  41:   archive_anchor: ArchiveAnchor
  42:   graph: {
  43:     nodes: MemoryGraphNode[]
  44:     edges: MemoryGraphEdge[]
  45:   }
  46: }
  47: 
  48: function formatKindLabel(value: string): string {
  49:   return value.replace(/_/g, ' ')
  50: }
  51: 
  52: function managedGraphMetadata(rootId: string): Record<string, string> {
  53:   return {
  54:     managed_by: TRANSCRIPT_MEMORY_EXTRACTOR,
  55:     managed_root_id: rootId,
  56:     managed_scope: 'session',
  57:   }
  58: }
  59: 
  60: function withManagedGraphProperties(
  61:   rootId: string,
  62:   properties: Record<string, unknown>,
  63: ): Record<string, unknown> {
  64:   return {
  65:     ...properties,
  66:     ...managedGraphMetadata(rootId),
  67:   }
  68: }
  69: 
  70: interface TranscriptUnitSegment {
  71:   title: string
  72:   summary: string
  73:   body: string
  74:   sourceRef: string
  75: }
  76: 
  77: function sessionNodeId(manifest: ManifestEntry): string {
  78:   return `session:${manifest.client}:${manifest.project}:${manifest.session_id}`
  79: }
  80: 
  81: function sourceAssetId(manifest: ManifestEntry): string {
  82:   return `source_asset:${manifest.client}:${manifest.project}:${manifest.session_id}:${manifest.raw_sha256.slice(0, 8)}`
  83: }
  84: 
  85: function archiveAnchorId(manifest: ManifestEntry): string {
  86:   return `archive_anchor:${manifest.client}:${manifest.project}:${manifest.session_id}:${manifest.raw_sha256.slice(0, 8)}`
  87: }
  88: 
  89: function sourcePartition(timestamp: string): string | undefined {
  90:   const [yearToken, monthToken] = timestampPartition(timestamp)
  91:   if (yearToken === 'unknown' || monthToken === 'unknown') return undefined
  92:   return `${yearToken}-${monthToken}`
  93: }
  94: 
  95: function formatRoleLabel(role: string): string {
  96:   const normalized = role.trim().toLowerCase()
  97:   switch (normalized) {
  98:     case 'user':
  99:       return 'User'
 100:     case 'assistant':
 101:       return 'Assistant'
 102:     default:
 103:       return normalized ? normalized[0]!.toUpperCase() + normalized.slice(1) : 'Message'
 104:   }
 105: }
 106: 
 107: function segmentMessages(messages: readonly TranscriptMessage[]): TranscriptMessage[][] {
 108:   const segments: TranscriptMessage[][] = []
 109:   let current: TranscriptMessage[] = []
 110: 
 111:   for (const message of messages) {
 112:     if (message.role === 'user' && current.length > 0) {
 113:       segments.push(current)
 114:       current = []
 115:     }
 116:     current.push(message)
 117:   }
 118: 
 119:   if (current.length > 0) {
 120:     segments.push(current)
 121:   }
 122: 
 123:   return segments
 124: }
 125: 
 126: function createTurnSegments(parsed: ParsedTranscript, manifest: ManifestEntry): TranscriptUnitSegment[] {
 127:   const baseTitle = manifest.title || manifest.session_id
 128:   const segments: TranscriptUnitSegment[] = segmentMessages(parsed.messages).map((messages, index) => {
 129:     const firstText = messages.find((message) => message.text.trim())?.text ?? baseTitle
 130:     const summary = truncate(firstText.split(/\r?\n/, 1)[0]!.trim() || baseTitle, 160)
 131:     const body = messages.map((message) => [
 132:       `${formatRoleLabel(message.role)}:`,
 133:       message.text.trim(),
 134:     ].join('\n')).join('\n\n')
 135: 
 136:     return {
 137:       title: `${baseTitle} Turn ${index + 1}`,
 138:       summary,
 139:       body,
 140:       sourceRef: `turn:${index + 1}`,
 141:     }
 142:   })
 143: 
 144:   if (parsed.tool_events.length > 0) {
 145:     const summary = truncate(parsed.tool_events[0]!.summary, 160)
 146:     segments.push({
 147:       title: `${baseTitle} Tools`,
 148:       summary,
 149:       body: parsed.tool_events
 150:         .map((event, index) => `${index + 1}. ${event.summary}`)
 151:         .join('\n'),
 152:       sourceRef: 'tools',
 153:     })
 154:   }
 155: 
 156:   if (segments.length === 0) {
 157:     segments.push({
 158:       title: baseTitle,
 159:       summary: truncate(baseTitle, 160),
 160:       body: baseTitle,
 161:       sourceRef: 'session',
 162:     })
 163:   }
 164: 
 165:   return segments
 166: }
 167: 
 168: export function buildTranscriptSourceAsset(
 169:   manifest: ManifestEntry,
 170:   cleanContent: string,
 171: ): SourceAsset {
 172:   const partition = sourcePartition(manifest.started_at)
 173:   return {
 174:     id: sourceAssetId(manifest),
 175:     asset_kind: 'transcript',
 176:     project: manifest.project,
 177:     ...(partition ? { partition } : {}),
 178:     title: manifest.title || manifest.session_id,
 179:     mime_type: 'text/markdown',
 180:     text_content: cleanContent,
 181:     source_uri: manifest.global_clean_path,
 182:     import_ref: manifest.global_manifest_path,
 183:     created_at: manifest.imported_at,
 184:     updated_at: manifest.imported_at,
 185:   }
 186: }
 187: 
 188: export function buildTranscriptExtractionBundle(
 189:   parsed: ParsedTranscript,
 190:   manifest: ManifestEntry,
 191:   cleanContent: string,
 192: ): MemoryExtractionBundle {
 193:   const sourceAsset = buildTranscriptSourceAsset(manifest, cleanContent)
 194:   const rawSegments = createTurnSegments(parsed, manifest)
 195:   const partition = sourceAsset.partition
 196:   const sessionRootId = sessionNodeId(manifest)
 197: 
 198:   const memoryUnits = rawSegments.map((segment, index): MemoryUnit => ({
 199:     id: `memory_unit:${manifest.client}:${manifest.project}:${manifest.session_id}:${String(index + 1).padStart(3, '0')}:${contentHash(segment.body)}`,
 200:     unit_type: 'document_chunk',
 201:     title: segment.title,
 202:     body: segment.body,
 203:     summary: segment.summary,
 204:     project: manifest.project,
 205:     ...(partition ? { partition } : {}),
 206:     source_kind: 'transcript',
 207:     source_id: sourceAsset.id,
 208:     source_ref: segment.sourceRef,
 209:     source_asset_ids: [sourceAsset.id],
 210:     confidence: 1,
 211:     weight: 1,
 212:     status: 'active',
 213:     created_at: manifest.imported_at,
 214:     updated_at: manifest.imported_at,
 215:   }))
 216: 
 217:   memoryUnits.forEach((unit, index) => {
 218:     const related: string[] = []
 219:     if (index > 0) related.push(memoryUnits[index - 1]!.id)
 220:     if (index < memoryUnits.length - 1) related.push(memoryUnits[index + 1]!.id)
 221:     if (related.length > 0) {
 222:       unit.related_unit_ids = related
 223:     }
 224:   })
 225: 
 226:   const archiveAnchor: ArchiveAnchor = {
 227:     id: archiveAnchorId(manifest),
 228:     scope: 'session',
 229:     title: manifest.title || manifest.session_id,
 230:     summary: truncate(
 231:       rawSegments
 232:         .map((segment, index) => `T${index + 1}: ${segment.summary}`)
 233:         .join(' | '),
 234:       320,
 235:     ),
 236:     project: manifest.project,
 237:     ...(partition ? { partition } : {}),
 238:     source_asset_ids: [sourceAsset.id],
 239:     memory_unit_ids: memoryUnits.map((unit) => unit.id),
 240:     created_at: manifest.imported_at,
 241:     updated_at: manifest.imported_at,
 242:   }
 243: 
 244:   const sessionNode: MemoryGraphNode = {
 245:     id: sessionRootId,
 246:     labels: ['Session'],
 247:     properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, {
 248:       entity_kind: 'session',
 249:       client: manifest.client,
 250:       project: manifest.project,
 251:       session_id: manifest.session_id,
 252:       title: manifest.title,
 253:       started_at: manifest.started_at,
 254:       cwd: manifest.cwd,
 255:       branch: manifest.branch,
 256:     }),
 257:   }
 258: 
 259:   const graphNodes: MemoryGraphNode[] = [
 260:     sessionNode,
 261:     {
 262:       id: sourceAsset.id,
 263:       labels: ['SourceAsset', 'Transcript'],
 264:       properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, {
 265:         entity_kind: 'source asset',
 266:         asset_kind: formatKindLabel(sourceAsset.asset_kind),
 267:         project: sourceAsset.project,
 268:         title: sourceAsset.title,
 269:         source_uri: sourceAsset.source_uri,
 270:         import_ref: sourceAsset.import_ref,
 271:       }),
 272:     },
 273:     {
 274:       id: archiveAnchor.id,
 275:       labels: ['ArchiveAnchor'],
 276:       properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, {
 277:         entity_kind: 'archive anchor',
 278:         scope: archiveAnchor.scope,
 279:         title: archiveAnchor.title,
 280:         summary: archiveAnchor.summary,
 281:       }),
 282:     },
 283:     ...memoryUnits.map((unit) => ({
 284:       id: unit.id,
 285:       labels: ['MemoryUnit', 'DocumentChunk'],
 286:       properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, {
 287:         entity_kind: 'memory unit',
 288:         unit_type: unit.unit_type,
 289:         unit_type_display: formatKindLabel(unit.unit_type),
 290:         project: unit.project,
 291:         title: unit.title,
 292:         summary: unit.summary,
 293:         source_ref: unit.source_ref,
 294:       }),
 295:     })),
 296:   ]
 297: 
 298:   const graphEdges: MemoryGraphEdge[] = [
 299:     {
 300:       from_id: sessionNode.id,
 301:       to_id: sourceAsset.id,
 302:       type: 'HAS_SOURCE_ASSET',
 303:       properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, {}),
 304:     },
 305:     {
 306:       from_id: sessionNode.id,
 307:       to_id: archiveAnchor.id,
 308:       type: 'HAS_ARCHIVE_ANCHOR',
 309:       properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, {}),
 310:     },
 311:     ...memoryUnits.flatMap((unit, index) => {
 312:       const edges: MemoryGraphEdge[] = [
 313:         {
 314:           from_id: sessionNode.id,
 315:           to_id: unit.id,
 316:           type: 'CONTAINS_UNIT',
 317:           properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, { position: index + 1 }),
 318:         },
 319:         {
 320:           from_id: sourceAsset.id,
 321:           to_id: unit.id,
 322:           type: 'EMITS_UNIT',
 323:           properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, { source_ref: unit.source_ref ?? '' }),
 324:         },
 325:         {
 326:           from_id: archiveAnchor.id,
 327:           to_id: unit.id,
 328:           type: 'SUMMARIZES',
 329:           properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, { position: index + 1 }),
 330:         },
 331:       ]
 332:       if (index < memoryUnits.length - 1) {
 333:         edges.push({
 334:           from_id: unit.id,
 335:           to_id: memoryUnits[index + 1]!.id,
 336:           type: 'NEXT_UNIT',
 337:           properties: withManagedGraphProperties(sessionRootId, {}),
 338:         })
 339:       }
 340:       return edges
 341:     }),
 342:   ]
 343: 
 344:   return {
 345:     extraction_version: TRANSCRIPT_MEMORY_EXTRACTION_VERSION,
 346:     extractor: TRANSCRIPT_MEMORY_EXTRACTOR,
 347:     generated_at: manifest.imported_at,
 348:     project: manifest.project,
 349:     session_id: manifest.session_id,
 350:     source_asset: sourceAsset,
 351:     memory_units: memoryUnits,
 352:     archive_anchor: archiveAnchor,
 353:     graph: {
 354:       nodes: graphNodes,
 355:       edges: graphEdges,
 356:     },
 357:   }
 358: }
 359: 
 360: function toAdapterGraphNode(node: MemoryGraphNode): AdapterGraphNode {
 361:   return {
 362:     id: node.id,
 363:     labels: node.labels,
 364:     properties: node.properties,
 365:   }
 366: }
 367: 
 368: function toAdapterGraphEdge(edge: MemoryGraphEdge): AdapterGraphEdge {
 369:   return {
 370:     fromId: edge.from_id,
 371:     toId: edge.to_id,
 372:     type: edge.type,
 373:     ...(edge.properties ? { properties: edge.properties } : {}),
 374:   }
 375: }
 376: 
 377: function resolveExtractionRootId(bundle: MemoryExtractionBundle): string {
 378:   const sessionNode = bundle.graph.nodes.find((node) =>
 379:     node.labels.includes('Session')
 380:       && node.properties['session_id'] === bundle.session_id)
 381: 
 382:   if (!sessionNode) {
 383:     throw new Error('MemoryExtractionBundle is missing its Session root node')
 384:   }
 385: 
 386:   return sessionNode.id
 387: }
 388: 
 389: export function syncExtractionBundleGraph(
 390:   graph: GraphAdapter,
 391:   bundle: MemoryExtractionBundle,
 392: ): void {
 393:   graph.deleteManagedSubgraph({
 394:     managedBy: bundle.extractor,
 395:     managedRootId: resolveExtractionRootId(bundle),
 396:   })
 397: 
 398:   for (const node of bundle.graph.nodes) {
 399:     graph.upsertNode(toAdapterGraphNode(node))
 400:   }
 401: 
 402:   for (const edge of bundle.graph.edges) {
 403:     graph.upsertEdge(toAdapterGraphEdge(edge))
 404:   }
 405: }

assistant #134 final_answer 07:35:05

做了两轮 review，发现 2 个需要修的点。

1. 高优先级：lineage 现在被写成了“最终表里不能为空”，但这会直接卡死第一批资产的落地。ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset 的首个版本通常是从原始 transcript、repo 证据或 import 产物生成的，它们天然没有前序资产可追溯，当前这条规则会让 genesis 资产无法 materialize。位置在 [OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md:805](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L805) 到 [L809](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L809)。

2. 中优先级：root_refs 和 asset_edges 都还缺少能支撑幂等重放的确定性规则。root_refs 说“最新一条就是当前视图头”，但 schema 没有 is_current / active 之类的标记，也没有把 root_kind + root_id + projection_version 约束成唯一键；asset_edges 只有 id 主键，却没规定如何稳定生成这个 id。这样一来，重复投影时很容易出现“当前头不唯一”或“重复边”的问题，和文档里强调的幂等 materialization 有冲突。位置在 [OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md:854](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L854) 到 [L919](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L919)。

如果你要，我下一步可以直接把这两处 contract 补严。

Show task summary

做了两轮 review，发现 2 个需要修的点。

1. 高优先级：lineage 现在被写成了“最终表里不能为空”，但这会直接卡死第一批资产的落地。ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset 的首个版本通常是从原始 transcript、repo 证据或 import 产物生成的，它们天然没有前序资产可追溯，当前这条规则会让 genesis 资产无法 materialize。位置在 [OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md:805](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L805) 到 [L809](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L809)。

2. 中优先级：root_refs 和 asset_edges 都还缺少能支撑幂等重放的确定性规则。root_refs 说“最新一条就是当前视图头”，但 schema 没有 is_current / active 之类的标记，也没有把 root_kind + root_id + projection_version 约束成唯一键；asset_edges 只有 id 主键，却没规定如何稳定生成这个 id。这样一来，重复投影时很容易出现“当前头不唯一”或“重复边”的问题，和文档里强调的幂等 materialization 有冲突。位置在 [OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md:854](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L854) 到 [L919](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L919)。

如果你要，我下一步可以直接把这两处 contract 补严。

Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",

    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: d:\demo1\openmnemo\Memory\04_knowledge\OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md
@@
 约束：
 
-- `lineage` 必须可追溯到至少一个前序资产，空值不允许进入最终表。
+- `lineage` 对非 genesis 资产必须可追溯到至少一个前序资产。
+- 首次 materialization 产生的 genesis 资产允许 `lineage` 为空，但必须通过 `source_refs` 或 `evidence_refs` 直接锚定事实来源。
 - `supersedes` 只描述版本关系，不描述业务依赖。
 - `search_text` 面向 FTS，`embedding_text` 面向向量层，二者不可互相替代。
@@
 ### 13.5 `asset_edges` 表
 
 `asset_edges` 负责长期复用关系，不负责树形导航。
+
+边 ID 必须可重复生成，推荐规则为稳定内容哈希前缀，例如：
+
+- `asset_edge:<hash(from_asset_id,to_asset_id,edge_type,project,scope_id,evidence_refs)>`
 
 建议字段：
@@
 ### 13.6 `root_refs` 与 `materialization_runs`
 
 `root_refs` 用来保存“某个根当前投影到了哪里”，`materialization_runs` 用来记录一次投影的输入、输出与状态。
+
+`root_refs` 必须按 `root_kind + root_id + scope_id + projection_version` 保持单行幂等 upsert；建议对该组合加唯一约束。
 
 #### `root_refs`
@@
 - `created_at TEXT NOT NULL`
 - `updated_at TEXT NOT NULL`
+
+建议唯一约束：
+
+- `(root_kind, root_id, scope_id, projection_version)`
+
+建议 `id` 生成规则：
+
+- `root_ref:<projection_version>:<root_kind>:<root_id>:<scope_id|global>`
@@
 #### `materialization_runs`
@@
 约束：
 
 - 同一个 `root_kind + root_id + projection_version` 的重复 materialization 必须幂等。
-- `root_refs` 里“最新一条”就是当前视图头，不允许靠外部约定猜测。
+- `root_refs` 不靠“最新一条”语义猜测当前头，而是靠唯一键幂等 upsert 直接定位当前视图头。
 - `materialization_runs` 只追加，不回写历史。

+- contract review 已修正两个关键点：genesis 资产允许空 `lineage`，并且 `root_refs` / `asset_edges` 已补齐幂等生成与唯一键约束。
 - 记忆压缩方案继续按照“全量原文保留 + 树形索引收敛 + 探针式逐层展开”原则推进。

@@
 Time: 2026-03-24 14:45
 User: Asked to continue the implementation contract work.
 Assistant: Re-read the active goal, bound todo, latest chat log, the current `DataLayerAPI` and extraction code, and the existing implementation-contract note. Then extended the contract from concept-level into table-level and behavior-level detail by adding concrete SQLite materialization guidance for `tree_nodes`, `assets`, `product_assets`, `memory_assets`, `skill_assets`, `asset_edges`, `root_refs`, and `materialization_runs`, plus explicit probe/runtime stop rules, raw hydration semantics, compatibility fallbacks, and failure codes. Updated the active todo to reflect that the contract now has enough detail for direct coding.
+
+---
+
+Time: 2026-03-24 14:55
+User: Requested two review passes.
+Assistant: Ran two review passes on the implementation contract. The first pass found that the contract would block first-generation assets because `lineage` was specified as non-empty for all assets, even though genesis assets only have raw evidence and no prior asset lineage. The second pass found that the idempotent materialization story was incomplete because `root_refs` lacked a unique-key rule and current-head generation rule, while `asset_edges` lacked a deterministic ID scheme. Fixed both issues by allowing empty lineage only for genesis assets with direct evidence anchors, and by adding deterministic `root_refs` / `asset_edges` generation and unique-key guidance to the contract. Updated the todo to reflect the tightened contract.
*** End Patch

886:### 13.6 `root_refs` 与 `materialization_runs`

- `updated_at TEXT NOT NULL`

约束：

- `lineage` 对非 genesis 资产必须可追溯到至少一个前序资产。
- 首次 materialization 产生的 genesis 资产允许 `lineage` 为空，但必须通过 `source_refs` 或 `evidence_refs` 直接锚定事实来源。
- `supersedes` 只描述版本关系，不描述业务依赖。
- `search_text` 面向 FTS，`embedding_text` 面向向量层，二者不可互相替代。

### 13.4 子类型详情表

子类型专有字段建议拆到独立详情表，避免 `assets` 基表膨胀。

#### `product_assets`

- `asset_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY`
- `product_kind TEXT NOT NULL`
- `repo_uri TEXT`
- `repo_path TEXT`
- `branch TEXT`
- `commit_range TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `artifact_format TEXT`
- `owner_module TEXT`
- `build_state TEXT NOT NULL`

#### `memory_assets`

- `asset_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY`
- `memory_kind TEXT NOT NULL`
- `root_kind TEXT NOT NULL`
- `root_id TEXT NOT NULL`
- `node_ref TEXT`
- `anchor_text TEXT`
- `raw_refs TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `parent_memory_id TEXT`
- `child_memory_ids TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `branch_key TEXT`
- `probe_policy TEXT`

#### `skill_assets`

- `asset_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY`
- `skill_kind TEXT NOT NULL`
- `trigger_conditions TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `prerequisites TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `steps TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `inputs TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `outputs TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `success_signals TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `anti_patterns TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `confidence REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0`

### 13.5 `asset_edges` 表

`asset_edges` 负责长期复用关系，不负责树形导航。

边 ID 必须可重复生成，推荐规则为稳定内容哈希前缀，例如：

- `asset_edge:<hash(from_asset_id,to_asset_id,edge_type,project,scope_id,evidence_refs)>`

建议字段：

- `id TEXT PRIMARY KEY`
- `from_asset_id TEXT NOT NULL`
- `to_asset_id TEXT NOT NULL`
- `edge_type TEXT NOT NULL`
- `project TEXT NOT NULL`
- `scope_id TEXT`
- `evidence_refs TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `weight REAL`
- `created_at TEXT NOT NULL`
- `updated_at TEXT NOT NULL`

建议边类型继续沿用：

- `LEADS_TO`
- `APPLIES_TO`
- `EXTRACTED_FROM`
- `EVOLVES_TO`
- `DEPENDS_ON`
- `EVIDENCED_BY`
- `MATERIALIZED_AS`

### 13.6 `root_refs` 与 `materialization_runs`

`root_refs` 用来保存“某个根当前投影到了哪里”，`materialization_runs` 用来记录一次投影的输入、输出与状态。

`root_refs` 必须按 `root_kind + root_id + scope_id + projection_version` 保持单行幂等 upsert；建议对该组合加唯一约束。

#### `root_refs`

- `id TEXT PRIMARY KEY`
- `root_kind TEXT NOT NULL`
- `root_id TEXT NOT NULL`
- `project TEXT NOT NULL`
- `scope_id TEXT`
- `projection_version TEXT NOT NULL`
- `head_node_id TEXT`
- `head_asset_id TEXT`
- `materialization_run_id TEXT`
- `privacy_level TEXT NOT NULL`
- `created_at TEXT NOT NULL`
- `updated_at TEXT NOT NULL`

建议唯一约束：

- `(root_kind, root_id, scope_id, projection_version)`

建议 `id` 生成规则：

- `root_ref:<projection_version>:<root_kind>:<root_id>:<scope_id|global>`

#### `materialization_runs`

- `id TEXT PRIMARY KEY`
- `projection_version TEXT NOT NULL`
- `root_kind TEXT NOT NULL`
- `root_id TEXT NOT NULL`
- `project TEXT NOT NULL`
- `scope_id TEXT`
- `input_source_refs TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `input_asset_ids TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`
- `status TEXT NOT NULL`
- `error_message TEXT`
- `started_at TEXT NOT NULL`
- `finished_at TEXT`

约束：

- 同一个 `root_kind + root_id + projection_version` 的重复 materialization 必须幂等。
- `root_refs` 不靠“最新一条”语义猜测当前头，而是靠唯一键幂等 upsert 直接定位当前视图头。
- `materialization_runs` 只追加，不回写历史。

---

## 14. Probe / runtime contract 补充

这一节把 probe 的停止条件、回退策略和兼容语义说得更硬一点，避免实现时各层各说各话。
Tool Result #155 shell_command call_EvaCm7IRNL0Yzk1EkUQ9Omcg exit 0 07:36:40
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.5 seconds Output: - contract review 已修正两个关键点：genesis 资产允许空 `lineage`，并且 `root_refs` / `asset_edges` 已补齐幂等生成与唯一键约束。 - 记忆压缩方案继续按照“全量原文保留 + 树形索引收敛 + 探针式逐层...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
- contract review 已修正两个关键点：genesis 资产允许空 `lineage`，并且 `root_refs` / `asset_edges` 已补齐幂等生成与唯一键约束。
- 记忆压缩方案继续按照“全量原文保留 + 树形索引收敛 + 探针式逐层展开”原则推进。
- 三类资产统一沉淀到用户自有 Git/Gitea 仓库中，保持 Git-native 定位。
- 当前 `DataLayerAPI` 继续以 `session / memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor / mixed` 作为运行时接口，三类资产模型先作为其上的投影层或聚合层落地。

## Deferred

- [ ] 完整 super agent harness
- [ ] 重型多 agent 编排 UI
- [ ] 企业权限与审计体系
- [ ] 插件市场 / skill 市场
- [ ] SaaS 托管闭环

assistant #158 final_answer 07:36:54

两轮 review 后，这 2 个问题已经修掉了。

- 高：lineage 原先被写成所有资产都必须非空，会卡住第一批 genesis 资产落表。现在已改成“非 genesis 资产必须可追溯，genesis 资产允许为空，但必须用 source_refs 或 evidence_refs 直接锚定事实来源”，见 [契约文档](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L807)。
- 中：root_refs 和 asset_edges 的幂等重放规则原先还不够硬。现在已补上 root_refs 的唯一键与确定性 id 规则，以及 asset_edges 的稳定哈希式 id 生成约定，见 [契约文档](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L861) 和 [契约文档](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md#L890)。

我也同步更新了 [todo](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/02_todos/todo_v003_001_20260324.md) 和 [chat log](d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/03_chat_logs/2026-03-24_13-15.md)。这轮复核后，没再看到新的阻塞性文档问题。

Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}
user #163 07:37:10

我要你深度的思考一个问题 你每次都问我 如果你要 如果你愿意 这是什么情况 是我们agents里规定的吗， 还是codex的现在的模型逻辑呢

reasoning #165 07:37:15

assistant #166 final_answer 07:37:17

这不是“agents 里硬性规定每句话都要这样”，也不是我必须固定用的模型口癖。更准确地说，它是三股东西叠在一起：

- 这套协作指令本身要求我“别让你替我做功课”“有分歧时温和地对齐”“有多个路径时先确认”。
- 我在不确定你是不是要继续、要改范围、还是要我直接执行时，会用“如果你要 / 如果你愿意”这种句式做低摩擦的衔接。
- 在你这个项目里，尤其是 MemoryTree / contract / review 这类工作，我常常需要把“我已经做了什么”和“下一步可不可以继续”分开说，所以这类收尾就出现得比较多。

所以结论是：不是强制规定，但确实是我当前的协作风格，而且和这套项目指令是兼容的。

如果你不喜欢这种说法，我可以直接切成更硬一点的风格：只给结论、发现、下一步，不再用“如果你要 / 如果你愿意”来试探收尾。

那套协作指令呢

token_count #172 07:38:45
total_tokens=1206710
total 1206710in 1180132out 26578reason 17660
reasoning #173 07:38:48

assistant #174 final_answer 07:38:50

那套协作指令本身，并没有要求我必须频繁说“如果你要 / 如果你愿意”。更准确地说，它在几个地方共同把我推向了这种表达方式：

- AGENTS.md 要求需要互动时用 plain-text questions，而且只在确实需要确认时才问。
- 开发指令要求我“只在 substantial 的时候才问澄清问题”，并且“尽量做合理假设”。
- 同时又要求我在有非显而易见后果时，温和地做对齐和升级，而不是直接拍板。

所以结果就是：我会倾向用一种保留式收尾，比如“如果你要，我可以继续”，来表示我已经把当前任务推进完了，但下一步还有多个可选路径。这个不是硬性规定，更像是我在这套协作规则下形成的默认语气。

如果你想要，我可以把以后风格改得更直接一点，少用这种试探式收尾，改成“结论 + 下一步选项”的硬风格。

total 1296793in 1269456out 27337reason 18176

那套协作指令本身，并没有要求我必须频繁说“如果你要 / 如果你愿意”。更准确地说，它在几个地方共同把我推向了这种表达方式：

- AGENTS.md 要求需要互动时用 plain-text questions，而且只在确实需要确认时才问。
- 开发指令要求我“只在 substantial 的时候才问澄清问题”，并且“尽量做合理假设”。
- 同时又要求我在有非显而易见后果时，温和地做对齐和升级，而不是直接拍板。

所以结果就是：我会倾向用一种保留式收尾，比如“如果你要，我可以继续”，来表示我已经把当前任务推进完了，但下一步还有多个可选路径。这个不是硬性规定，更像是我在这套协作规则下形成的默认语气。

如果你想要，我可以把以后风格改得更直接一点，少用这种试探式收尾，改成“结论 + 下一步选项”的硬风格。

task_started

  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

# Context from my IDE setup:

## Active file: AGENTS.md

## Open tabs:
- AGENTS.md: AGENTS.md

## My request for Codex:
这个是memorytree-workflow这个skill的要求 还是我们原本agent的要求呢

token_count #180 07:43:21
total_tokens=1296793
total 1296793in 1269456out 27337reason 18176
reasoning #181 07:43:24
Private reasoning captured; content is encrypted.

Private reasoning captured. Raw content is encrypted and not displayed.

assistant #182 final_answer 07:43:26

不是 memorytree-workflow 这个 skill 规定你一定会听到“如果你要 / 如果你愿意”这种话。

更准确地说：

- memorytree-workflow 只要求我在需要互动提问时，用 plain-text question，不依赖客户端 UI。
- 它还要求我在需要确认范围、heartbeat 设置、或会话连续性时，提出清晰的问题。
- 但它没有规定必须用“如果你要”这种句式。

你看到的这种表达，主要来自更上层的通用协作风格和 agent 指令：

- 只有在确实需要确认时才问。
- 尽量做合理假设，不把问题甩回给你。
- 需要升级或对齐时，要温和、可协作，而不是生硬打断。

所以结论是：

- “要不要问你”这件事，部分来自 memorytree-workflow 的互动要求。
- “用这种柔和收尾措辞”主要是原本 agent 的协作风格，不是 skill 的硬性要求。

如果你想，我后面可以直接改成更干脆的风格，不再用这种缓冲式句子。

不是 memorytree-workflow 这个 skill 规定你一定会听到“如果你要 / 如果你愿意”这种话。

更准确地说：

- memorytree-workflow 只要求我在需要互动提问时，用 plain-text question，不依赖客户端 UI。
- 它还要求我在需要确认范围、heartbeat 设置、或会话连续性时，提出清晰的问题。
- 但它没有规定必须用“如果你要”这种句式。

你看到的这种表达，主要来自更上层的通用协作风格和 agent 指令：

- 只有在确实需要确认时才问。
- 尽量做合理假设，不把问题甩回给你。
- 需要升级或对齐时，要温和、可协作，而不是生硬打断。

所以结论是：

- “要不要问你”这件事，部分来自 memorytree-workflow 的互动要求。
- “用这种柔和收尾措辞”主要是原本 agent 的协作风格，不是 skill 的硬性要求。

如果你想，我后面可以直接改成更干脆的风格，不再用这种缓冲式句子。

assistant #190 final_answer 07:48:40

接下来最该做的是把这份 contract 开始落代码，而不是再继续补文档。当前优先级我建议是：

1. 先做 packages/types 的类型落地
把 TreeNodeRecord / AssetRecord / AssetLayerAPI、以及它们依赖的 RootRefRecord、Probe*Request、ProbeResponse 先定下来。这个是后面 schema 和实现的共同入口。

2. 再做 index/asset-layer.sqlite 的 materialized schema
先把 tree_nodes / assets / asset_edges / root_refs / materialization_runs 建出来，并把唯一键、幂等 upsert 规则钉死。

3. 然后做 session 级投影
先把 transcript bundle 稳定投影成 conversation_leaf / archive_batch / session_milestone，这样可以验证整条链路。

4. 最后再挂第一版 AssetLayerAPI
让它先包在现有 DataLayerAPI 外面，保持旧接口不动。

如果按“最快推进、最少返工”来排，我建议今天就先从第 1 步开始。对应的当前收口状态在 [todo](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/02_todos/todo_v003_001_20260324.md) 里已经写清了，契约细节在 [实现级契约文档](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md) 里也已经足够直接开工。

Show task summary

接下来最该做的是把这份 contract 开始落代码，而不是再继续补文档。当前优先级我建议是：

1. 先做 packages/types 的类型落地
把 TreeNodeRecord / AssetRecord / AssetLayerAPI、以及它们依赖的 RootRefRecord、Probe*Request、ProbeResponse 先定下来。这个是后面 schema 和实现的共同入口。

2. 再做 index/asset-layer.sqlite 的 materialized schema
先把 tree_nodes / assets / asset_edges / root_refs / materialization_runs 建出来，并把唯一键、幂等 upsert 规则钉死。

3. 然后做 session 级投影
先把 transcript bundle 稳定投影成 conversation_leaf / archive_batch / session_milestone，这样可以验证整条链路。

4. 最后再挂第一版 AssetLayerAPI
让它先包在现有 DataLayerAPI 外面，保持旧接口不动。

如果按“最快推进、最少返工”来排，我建议今天就先从第 1 步开始。对应的当前收口状态在 [todo](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/02_todos/todo_v003_001_20260324.md) 里已经写清了，契约细节在 [实现级契约文档](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/Memory/04_knowledge/OpenMnemo_创造资产内核实现级契约_v1_2026-03-24.md) 里也已经足够直接开工。

Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",

    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }

我已经按最稳妥的方式处理了：从 main 切出了专门的分支 memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main，并把这批 MemoryTree 文档提交成了一个独立 commit：7e325b2，提交信息是 memorytree(openmnemo): tighten implementation contract。

现在的状态是：

- main 还停在 4bd91fd
- 当前分支比 main 前进了 1 个 MemoryTree 提交
- 这批 Memory/01-04 的 Markdown 文档已经进提交
- 仍然有 3 个根目录下的未跟踪资源没纳入这次提交：
- Memory/chat-memo_62_20260322075923/
- Memory/doubao_20260316153420_...OpenClaw...txt
- Memory/doubao_20260321101654_https_github_com_openmnemo_o.txt

我先没把那 3 个一起提交，因为它们更像 transcript / source 资源，归属边界还不够干净。现在这条 MemoryTree 分支可以直接继续往前走，或者如果你要，我下一步可以帮你把它整理成可合并到 main 的 PR 形态。

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}
user #271 07:59:47

# Context from my IDE setup:

## Active file: AGENTS.md

## Open tabs:
- AGENTS.md: AGENTS.md

## My request for Codex:
啥意思啊 为什么新建这个分支呢 我们memorytree-workflow这个skill不是规定再仓库内建立memorytree命名的分支吗 为什么是这个命名呢memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main

token_count #272 08:02:01
total_tokens=2333250
total 2333250in 2297431out 35819reason 24293
reasoning #273 08:02:06
Private reasoning captured; content is encrypted.

Private reasoning captured. Raw content is encrypted and not displayed.

assistant #274 final_answer 08:02:08

因为我把这批改动当成了 MemoryTree-only 的独立提交流来处理，所以先从 main 切了一个专门分支。这个做法来自 AGENTS.md 和 memorytree-workflow 的共同方向: MemoryTree 变化要尽量隔离成独立分支、独立提交，别和产品分支混在一起。

但你说得对，分支名字是我自己选的，不是 skill 强制要求的精确格式。我这里用 memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main 的原因只有两个：

- memorytree/ 前缀表示这是 MemoryTree 管理的分支
- openmnemo-contract-main 表示这条分支是从 main 切出来、做 contract 收口的

如果你觉得这个名字太长、太怪，我同意，完全可以改得更干净，比如：

- memorytree/openmnemo
- memorytree/openmnemo-contract
- memorytree/contract-main

所以结论是：

- 新建分支这件事是对的，因为要把 MemoryTree-only 改动隔离开
- 这个命名不是必须的，是我临时选的
- 如果你想要，我可以现在就把它改成你更顺手的命名

如果你要，我下一步可以直接给你把它改成更短的 memorytree/openmnemo。

total_tokens=2429950
total 2429950in 2393288out 36662reason 24809

因为我把这批改动当成了 MemoryTree-only 的独立提交流来处理，所以先从 main 切了一个专门分支。这个做法来自 AGENTS.md 和 memorytree-workflow 的共同方向: MemoryTree 变化要尽量隔离成独立分支、独立提交，别和产品分支混在一起。

但你说得对，分支名字是我自己选的，不是 skill 强制要求的精确格式。我这里用 memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main 的原因只有两个：

- memorytree/ 前缀表示这是 MemoryTree 管理的分支
- openmnemo-contract-main 表示这条分支是从 main 切出来、做 contract 收口的

如果你觉得这个名字太长、太怪，我同意，完全可以改得更干净，比如：

- memorytree/openmnemo
- memorytree/openmnemo-contract
- memorytree/contract-main

所以结论是：

- 新建分支这件事是对的，因为要把 MemoryTree-only 改动隔离开
- 这个命名不是必须的，是我临时选的
- 如果你想要，我可以现在就把它改成你更顺手的命名

如果你要，我下一步可以直接给你把它改成更短的 memorytree/openmnemo。

Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions.   "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

# Context from my IDE setup:

## Active file: AGENTS.md

## Open tabs:
- AGENTS.md: AGENTS.md

## My request for Codex:
为什么分支不能叫memorytree呢

token_count #280 08:07:01
total_tokens=2429950
total 2429950in 2393288out 36662reason 24809
reasoning #281 08:07:05
Private reasoning captured; content is encrypted.

Private reasoning captured. Raw content is encrypted and not displayed.

assistant #282 final_answer 08:07:08

可以，完全可以叫 memorytree。我刚才说“可以改短”，不是因为技术上不能，而是因为我当时用了一个更保守的命名。

为什么我没直接用 memorytree，主要是这几个考虑：

- memorytree 太泛了，放在多仓库或多 worktree 场景里，容易看不出它对应哪个项目。
- 这类分支通常会长期存在，带上项目名或用途后，后面维护、找分支、开 PR 都更清楚。
- 我想表达“这是从 main 切出来、专门放 contract 收口的 MemoryTree 分支”，所以用了更长的名字。

但从规则上讲，没有任何硬性限制说它不能叫 memorytree。如果你想要最简洁的命名，我们现在就可以把它改成：

- memorytree

或者如果你更想保留一点含义，也可以改成：

- memorytree/openmnemo
- memorytree/contract

我个人建议如果这是这个仓库里唯一的 MemoryTree 工作分支，直接叫 memorytree 也没问题。

可以，完全可以叫 memorytree。我刚才说“可以改短”，不是因为技术上不能，而是因为我当时用了一个更保守的命名。

为什么我没直接用 memorytree，主要是这几个考虑：

- memorytree 太泛了，放在多仓库或多 worktree 场景里，容易看不出它对应哪个项目。
- 这类分支通常会长期存在，带上项目名或用途后，后面维护、找分支、开 PR 都更清楚。
- 我想表达“这是从 main 切出来、专门放 contract 收口的 MemoryTree 分支”，所以用了更长的名字。

但从规则上讲，没有任何硬性限制说它不能叫 memorytree。如果你想要最简洁的命名，我们现在就可以把它改成：

- memorytree

或者如果你更想保留一点含义，也可以改成：

- memorytree/openmnemo
- memorytree/contract

我个人建议如果这是这个仓库里唯一的 MemoryTree 工作分支，直接叫 memorytree 也没问题。

# Context from my IDE setup:

## Active file: AGENTS.md

## Open tabs:
- AGENTS.md: AGENTS.md

## My request for Codex:
我是说你的命名是遵循memorytree-workflow这个skill 还是你自己的决策

token_count #288 08:12:36
total_tokens=2526964
total 2526964in 2489520out 37444reason 25325
reasoning #289 08:12:38
Private reasoning captured; content is encrypted.

Private reasoning captured. Raw content is encrypted and not displayed.

assistant #290 final_answer 08:12:40

是我自己的命名决策，不是 memorytree-workflow 这个 skill 强制要求的精确格式。

这个 skill 要求的是：

- MemoryTree-only 改动尽量放在独立分支里
- 用 MemoryTree-scoped commit title
- 不要把 MemoryTree 改动和产品代码混在一起

但它没有规定分支名必须叫 memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main，也没有规定必须带项目名或 -main 后缀。那是我为了更清楚地表达“从 main 切出、专门做 contract 收口、属于 OpenMnemo”的个人命名选择。

所以结论很直接：

- memorytree 这个名字可以
- memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main 不是 skill 规定的
- 它只是我为了可读性和区分度做的保守命名

如果你想，我可以把它改成更简洁的 memorytree。

- MemoryTree-only 改动尽量放在独立分支里

assistant #308 final_answer 08:16:42

有，main 里还没合并进来的分支现在至少有 3 个：

- feat/aichat-phase0-5
- memorytree/openmnemo
- memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main，就是我们当前所在的这个分支

其中最关键的是：

- memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main 是我刚刚从 main 切出来、还没合并回去的 MemoryTree 分支
- memorytree/openmnemo 也还是没并到 main，而且它比 origin/memorytree/openmnemo 还 ahead 1 个提交
- feat/aichat-phase0-5 也是独立于 main 的产品分支，当前也没合并

如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续帮你判断：
- 哪些分支该保留
- 哪些应该尽快提 PR 合进 main
- 哪些可以先不管

Show task summary

有，main 里还没合并进来的分支现在至少有 3 个：

- feat/aichat-phase0-5
- memorytree/openmnemo
- memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main，就是我们当前所在的这个分支

其中最关键的是：

- memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main 是我刚刚从 main 切出来、还没合并回去的 MemoryTree 分支
- memorytree/openmnemo 也还是没并到 main，而且它比 origin/memorytree/openmnemo 还 ahead 1 个提交
- feat/aichat-phase0-5 也是独立于 main 的产品分支，当前也没合并

如果你愿意，我下一步可以继续帮你判断：
- 哪些分支该保留
- 哪些应该尽快提 PR 合进 main
- 哪些可以先不管

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"

  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #328 final_answer 08:19:03

结论很明确：

- memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main 这条分支，适合尽快合进 main。它是我们刚刚整理出来的、只放 Memory/01-04 的 MemoryTree contract 文档提交，边界最干净。
- feat/aichat-phase0-5 不建议和这条 MemoryTree 线混着合。它是产品功能分支，应该走自己的产品 PR/合并节奏。
- memorytree/openmnemo 先别直接合进 main。它看起来是 MemoryTree 的工作分支，里面有大量 transcript / 导入 / 生成物改动，属于“工作流数据和仓库记忆”的范畴，适合继续保留，不适合现在无脑并入主线。

所以如果按“该留/该并”来分：

- 留着继续干活：memorytree/openmnemo
- 准备合到 main：memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main
- 产品线继续独立：feat/aichat-phase0-5

如果你要，我下一步可以直接帮你把 memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main 合到 main，然后再把当前还没纳入的那 3 个根目录 transcript 资源单独判断要不要保留。

结论很明确：

- memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main 这条分支，适合尽快合进 main。它是我们刚刚整理出来的、只放 Memory/01-04 的 MemoryTree contract 文档提交，边界最干净。
- feat/aichat-phase0-5 不建议和这条 MemoryTree 线混着合。它是产品功能分支，应该走自己的产品 PR/合并节奏。
- memorytree/openmnemo 先别直接合进 main。它看起来是 MemoryTree 的工作分支，里面有大量 transcript / 导入 / 生成物改动，属于“工作流数据和仓库记忆”的范畴，适合继续保留，不适合现在无脑并入主线。

所以如果按“该留/该并”来分：

- 留着继续干活：memorytree/openmnemo
- 准备合到 main：memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main
- 产品线继续独立：feat/aichat-phase0-5

如果你要，我下一步可以直接帮你把 memorytree/openmnemo-contract-main 合到 main，然后再把当前还没纳入的那 3 个根目录 transcript 资源单独判断要不要保留。

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #367 final_answer 08:46:47

feat/aichat-phase0-5 也还没合进 main，而且它和我们刚合进去的 MemoryTree 线不是一类东西。

现在它的状态更像是：

- 这是一个产品功能分支，内容主要是 chat / report / provider / types 这一套
- 它比当前 main 早，而且不包含我们刚合进去的 MemoryTree 文档提交
- 所以它适合单独走产品线，不适合和 MemoryTree contract 混成一个合并动作

更具体一点，正确节奏应该是：

- 让 feat/aichat-phase0-5 先同步当前 main
- 处理可能的冲突或差异
- 再按产品 PR 的方式单独合并

所以结论是：
它不是“不要合并”，而是“要单独合并，别和 MemoryTree 线混在一起”。

如果你要，我可以下一步直接帮你判断这条产品分支和当前 main 的具体冲突风险。

feat/aichat-phase0-5 也还没合进 main，而且它和我们刚合进去的 MemoryTree 线不是一类东西。

现在它的状态更像是：

- 这是一个产品功能分支，内容主要是 chat / report / provider / types 这一套
- 它比当前 main 早，而且不包含我们刚合进去的 MemoryTree 文档提交
- 所以它适合单独走产品线，不适合和 MemoryTree contract 混成一个合并动作

更具体一点，正确节奏应该是：

- 让 feat/aichat-phase0-5 先同步当前 main
- 处理可能的冲突或差异
- 再按产品 PR 的方式单独合并

所以结论是：
它不是“不要合并”，而是“要单独合并，别和 MemoryTree 线混在一起”。

如果你要，我可以下一步直接帮你判断这条产品分支和当前 main 的具体冲突风险。

common diff options:
  -z            output diff-raw with lines terminated with NUL.
  -p            output patch format.
  -u            synonym for -p.
  --patch-with-raw
                output both a patch and the diff-raw format.
  --stat        show diffstat instead of patch.
  --numstat     show numeric diffstat instead of patch.
  --patch-with-stat
                output a patch and prepend its diffstat.
  --name-only   show only names of changed files.
  --name-status show names and status of changed files.
  --full-index  show full object name on index lines.
  --abbrev=<n>  abbreviate object names in diff-tree header and diff-raw.
  -R            swap input file pairs.
  -B            detect complete rewrites.
  -M            detect renames.
  -C            detect copies.
  --find-copies-harder
                try unchanged files as candidate for copy detection.
  -l<n>         limit rename attempts up to <n> paths.
  -O<file>      reorder diffs according to the <file>.
  -S<string>    find filepair whose only one side contains the string.
  --pickaxe-all
                show all files diff when -S is used and hit is found.
  -a  --text    treat all files as text.

assistant #385 final_answer 08:50:29

冲突风险很低，基本可以判断为“文件路径上不打架”。

我刚对照了 main 和 feat/aichat-phase0-5 的共同基线 4bd91fd：

- main 这边新增的是 MemoryTree 文档，集中在 Memory/01-04
- feat/aichat-phase0-5 这边改的是产品代码，集中在 packages/cli、packages/core、packages/report、packages/types
- 两边几乎没有同路径改动，所以从 Git 合并角度看，不会像同一个文件被两边同时改那样产生典型冲突

真正需要注意的不是“合并冲突”，而是“集成时的行为一致性”：

- feat/aichat-phase0-5 里有一套 chat / report / provider 相关的新代码
- 它可能依赖当前 main 上已有的 core 结构，但这类风险通常是编译或测试问题，不是 merge conflict
- 目前看不到和刚合进 main 的 MemoryTree 文档线之间的路径冲突

所以结论是：

- 合并风险：低
- 直接冲突风险：几乎没有
- 更可能遇到的是测试或接口对齐问题，而不是 Git 冲突

如果你要，我下一步可以继续帮你做一个更实用的判断：这条 feat/aichat-phase0-5 是适合先 rebase 到当前 main，还是直接开 PR 让它自动合并。
