Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #423 final_answer 08:55:48

结果已经验证过了：

- 我做了临时分支 rebase 演练，feat/aichat-phase0-5 在当前 main 上 3 个提交全部无冲突。
- 所以技术上两条路都可行：直接 PR 或 先 rebase 再 PR。

建议方案：

1. 默认走 直接 PR（更稳，不改写已发布分支历史）。
2. 仅在你们仓库要求线性历史时，才走 rebase + force-with-lease。

当前还要注意一个前置事实：

- 本地 main 比 origin/main 领先 1 个提交（就是刚合进去的 MemoryTree contract）。
- 先把本地 main push 到远端，再处理 feat/aichat-phase0-5 会更干净。

结论：
feat/aichat-phase0-5 不需要因为冲突而强制 rebase；冲突风险已验证为低。直接走产品 PR 流程是合理选择。

Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.4 seconds Output: name: CI on: push: branches: [main] pull_request: branches: [main] jobs: build-and-test: runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }} strategy: fail-fas...
Show output payload

Output:
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
        node: [22, 24]

    env:
      TURBO_TELEMETRY_DISABLED: "1"

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd  # v6.0.2

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@fc06bc1257f339d1d5d8b3a19a8cae5388b55320  # v4.4.0

      - uses: actions/setup-node@53b83947a5a98c8d113130e565377fae1a50d02f  # v6.3.0
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
          cache: pnpm

      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - run: pnpm build

      - run: pnpm typecheck

      - run: pnpm lint

      - run: pnpm test

Tool Result #502 shell_command call_U0wgUvGhdu88osu44UcH2mYL exit 0 09:08:55

Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.4 seconds
Output:
import { afterEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest'

import { LLMProviderError } from '../../src/chat/llm-provider.js'
import { OpenAICompatibleChatProvider } from '../../src/chat/providers/openai-compatible.js'

function createStream(chunks: string[]): ReadableStream<Uint8Array> {
  const encoder = new TextEncoder()
  let index = 0
  return new ReadableStream<Uint8Array>({
    pull(controller) {
      if (index >= chunks.length) {
        controller.close()
        return
      }
      controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(chunks[index]!))
      index += 1
    },
  })
}

async function collectEvents(provider: OpenAICompatibleChatProvider) {
  const events = []
  for await (const event of provider.stream({
    system: 'You answer from memory.',
    messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Hello relay' }],
    model: 'openai/gpt-4.1-mini',
  })) {
    events.push(event)
  }
  return events
}

describe('OpenAICompatibleChatProvider', () => {
  afterEach(() => {
    vi.unstubAllGlobals()
  })

  it('streams delta events from an OpenAI-compatible SSE endpoint', async () => {
    const fetchMock = vi.fn(async (_url: string, init?: RequestInit) => new Response(
      createStream([
        'data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":"Hello"}}]}\n\n',
        'data: {"choices":[{"delta":{"content":" world"},"finish_reason":"stop"}]}\n\n',
        'data: [DONE]\n\n',
      ]),
      {
        status: 200,
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'text/event-stream' },
      },
    ))
    vi.stubGlobal('fetch', fetchMock)

    const provider = new OpenAICompatibleChatProvider({
      apiKey: 'relay-key',
      baseUrl: 'https://relay.example.com/v1',
      defaultModel: 'openai/gpt-4.1-mini',
    })
    const events = await collectEvents(provider)

    expect(fetchMock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
    const [url, init] = fetchMock.mock.calls[0]!
    expect(url).toBe('https://relay.example.com/v1/chat/completions')
    expect(init?.method).toBe('POST')
    expect(JSON.parse(String(init?.body))).toMatchObject({
      model: 'openai/gpt-4.1-mini',
      stream: true,
      max_tokens: 1024,
      messages: [
        { role: 'system', content: 'You answer from memory.' },
        { role: 'user', content: 'Hello relay' },
      ],
    })
    expect(events).toEqual([
      { type: 'delta', text: 'Hello' },
      { type: 'delta', text: ' world' },
      { type: 'done', reason: 'stop' },
    ])
  })

  it('supports non-stream JSON relay responses', async () => {
    vi.stubGlobal('fetch', vi.fn(async () => new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        choices: [
          {
            message: {
              content: 'Hello from JSON relay',
            },
            finish_reason: 'stop',
          },
        ],
      }),
      {
        status: 200,
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json; charset=utf-8' },
      },
    )))

    const provider = new OpenAICompatibleChatProvider({
      apiKey: 'relay-key',
      baseUrl: 'https://relay.example.com/v1',
      defaultModel: 'openai/gpt-4.1-mini',
    })

    const events = await collectEvents(provider)

    expect(events).toEqual([
      { type: 'delta', text: 'Hello from JSON relay' },
      { type: 'done', reason: 'stop' },
    ])
  })

  it('surfaces relay HTTP errors as provider errors', async () => {
    vi.stubGlobal('fetch', vi.fn(async () => new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: {
          message: 'Invalid relay key.',
          code: 'invalid_api_key',
        },
      }),
      {
        status: 401,
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      },
    )))

    const provider = new OpenAICompatibleChatProvider({
      apiKey: 'bad-key',
      baseUrl: 'https://relay.example.com/v1',
    })

    await expect(collectEvents(provider)).rejects.toEqual(
      expect.objectContaining<Partial<LLMProviderError>>({
        message: 'Invalid relay key.',
        code: 'invalid_api_key',
      }),
    )
  })
})

assistant #510 final_answer 09:09:33

有 CI，不是没有。现在 PR #13 的 CI 已经跑了，但 4 个矩阵任务都在 pnpm lint 步骤失败，所以后面的 pnpm test 被跳过了。

关键信息：

- CI workflow 存在并启用：[ci.yml](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/.github/workflows/ci.yml)
- 触发条件是 push main 和 pull_request -> main
- 失败点是 lint 规则：@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
- 具体报错位置在 feature 分支文件第 39 行的 init 未使用（应改为 _init 或移除）
参考路径：[openai-compatible-provider.test.ts](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/tests/chat/openai-compatible-provider.test.ts)（该文件在 feat/aichat-phase0-5 分支上）

E2E 这块目前看是“没有独立配置”：

- .github/workflows 里只有 ci.yml / publish.yml / release.yml
- 根 package.json 也没有单独 e2e 脚本：[package.json](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/package.json)

所以结论是：有 CI、无独立 E2E；当前是 lint 挂掉，不是没跑 CI。

有 CI，不是没有。现在 PR #13 的 CI 已经跑了，但 4 个矩阵任务都在 pnpm lint 步骤失败，所以后面的 pnpm test 被跳过了。

关键信息：

- CI workflow 存在并启用：[ci.yml](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/.github/workflows/ci.yml)
- 触发条件是 push main 和 pull_request -> main
- 失败点是 lint 规则：@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
- 具体报错位置在 feature 分支文件第 39 行的 init 未使用（应改为 _init 或移除）
参考路径：[openai-compatible-provider.test.ts](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/tests/chat/openai-compatible-provider.test.ts)（该文件在 feat/aichat-phase0-5 分支上）

E2E 这块目前看是“没有独立配置”：

- .github/workflows 里只有 ci.yml / publish.yml / release.yml
- 根 package.json 也没有单独 e2e 脚本：[package.json](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/package.json)

所以结论是：有 CI、无独立 E2E；当前是 lint 挂掉，不是没跑 CI。

Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",

    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000

assistant #578 final_answer 09:15:27

已按“源头修复”处理完，不是临时绕过。

根因是 eslint 规则 @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars，在测试文件里有一个未使用参数 init。我把它改成 _init（明确标记为有意未使用），这样保留了测试语义，也符合现有 lint 规则，不需要放宽规则或关掉检查。
修复文件：[openai-compatible-provider.test.ts](/d:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/tests/chat/openai-compatible-provider.test.ts)

我还做了两层验证：

- pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core lint 通过
- pnpm lint（全仓 turbo lint）通过

然后我已提交并推送到 PR 分支：

- commit: cbf6097
- 分支: feat/aichat-phase0-5
- PR #13 的 CI 已重新触发，当前是 4 个矩阵任务 pending（新一轮在跑）

已按“源头修复”处理完，不是临时绕过。

根因是 eslint 规则 @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars，在测试文件里有一个未使用参数 init。我把它改成 _init（明确标记为有意未使用），这样保留了测试语义，也符合现有 lint 规则，不需要放宽规则或关掉检查。

我还做了两层验证：

- pnpm lint（全仓 turbo lint）通过

然后我已提交并推送到 PR 分支：

Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #611 commentary 10:00:23

我把 PR 描述升级成一版完整审阅稿（背景、范围、设计决策、测试、风险、review 指南），并把刚才的 lint 修复也写进说明里。现在直接更新 PR 正文。

Tool Call #612 shell_command call_TRXQ6gwUfu1J4D6JzPYzggD2 10:00:44
shell_command input={"command":"@'\n## 背景与目标\n本 PR 落地 AI Chat Phase 0.5 的产品能力，目标是把“本地聊天 + 可持久化会话 + 可切换模型中继（relay）+ 报告页可视化”串成一条可用链路，同时保持与现有 Memory/DataLayer 主线解耦。\n\n核心目标：\n- 提供可运行的本地 Chat 流程（非一次性 d...
Show input payload 
{
  "command": "@'\n## 背景与目标\n本 PR 落地 AI Chat Phase 0.5 的产品能力，目标是把“本地聊天 + 可持久化会话 + 可切换模型中继（relay）+ 报告页可视化”串成一条可用链路，同时保持与现有 Memory/DataLayer 主线解耦。\n\n核心目标：\n- 提供可运行的本地 Chat 流程（非一次性 demo）\n- 引入 provider 抽象，支持 OpenAI-compatible relay 与 Anthropic 两类接入\n- 支持会话持久化、上下文构建和引用回链\n- 在 report 端提供可浏览的 chat UI 渲染\n\n## 本次改动范围\n### 1) Types 层\n- 新增 `packages/types/src/chat.ts`\n- 扩展 `packages/types/src/index.ts` 暴露 chat 类型\n- 统一 chat 相关输入输出结构，避免 core/cli/report 各自定义\n\n### 2) Core 层（chat runtime）\n新增模块：\n- `packages/core/src/chat/chat-service.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/context-builder.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/conversation.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/llm-provider.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/local-chat-service.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/prompt.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/provider-factory.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/providers/openai-compatible.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/providers/anthropic.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/index.ts`\n\n关键能力：\n- 将 chat 逻辑分为 provider 调用、上下文组装、会话读写、服务编排\n- 支持 SSE 流式与 JSON 非流式 relay 响应\n- 将 relay 配置与调用逻辑解耦（baseUrl/apiKey/model）\n- 输出结构化事件流，便于 CLI 与 report 共用\n\n### 3) CLI 层\n- 更新 `packages/cli/src/cmd-report.ts`\n- 新增 `packages/cli/tests/cli/report-chat.test.ts`\n\n作用：\n- 将 chat 结果纳入报告生成流程\n- 保持 CLI 调用链与 core chat service 对齐\n\n### 4) Report 层（UI 渲染）\n新增：\n- `packages/report/src/chat/frontend-adapter.ts`\n- `packages/report/src/chat/ui.ts`\n- `packages/report/src/render/chat.ts`\n并更新：\n- `packages/report/src/render/layout.ts`\n- `packages/report/tests/build.test.ts`\n- `packages/report/tests/chat-frontend-adapter.test.ts`\n\n作用：\n- 在报告侧提供 chat 展示能力\n- 保证渲染和构建流程可测试\n\n### 5) 其他\n- `packages/core/src/storage/vector/sqlite-vec-adapter.ts` 小改动（与 chat 线集成相关）\n- `pnpm-lock.yaml` 更新依赖锁\n- 增加/更新 chat 相关测试（core/report/cli）\n\n## 架构与边界说明\n- 本 PR 是产品能力线（AI Chat Phase 0.5），不是 MemoryTree-only 文档线。\n- 与 MemoryTree contract 文档更新分离提交、分离合并，避免产品代码与仓库记忆管理变更混杂。\n- 不在本 PR 内引入额外 SaaS/权限/多 agent 编排等上层能力。\n\n## 测试与验证\n本地验证：\n- `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core lint` ✅\n- `pnpm lint`（turbo 全仓）✅\n\nCI（PR #13）验证：\n- `build-and-test (ubuntu-latest, 22)` ✅\n- `build-and-test (ubuntu-latest, 24)` ✅\n- `build-and-test (windows-latest, 22)` ✅\n- `build-and-test (windows-latest, 24)` ✅\n\n说明：\n- 本 PR 过程中曾出现 `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars`（测试文件未使用参数）导致 lint 失败，已在 `fix(core-tests): satisfy no-unused-vars in relay provider test` 修复。\n\n## Reviewer Guide（建议审阅顺序）\n1. 看类型定义：`packages/types/src/chat.ts`\n2. 看 core provider 抽象与实现：`packages/core/src/chat/llm-provider.ts` + `providers/*`\n3. 看 service 编排与会话持久化：`chat-service.ts` / `conversation.ts`\n4. 看 CLI 接线：`packages/cli/src/cmd-report.ts`\n5. 看 report 渲染：`packages/report/src/chat/*` 与 `render/chat.ts`\n6. 最后看测试覆盖：`packages/core/tests/chat/*`、`packages/report/tests/*chat*`、`packages/cli/tests/cli/report-chat.test.ts`\n\n## 后续工作（不在本 PR）\n- 更完整的 provider 能力矩阵与错误码规范化\n- 更细粒度的会话/引用权限策略\n- 与后续资产层（AssetLayerAPI）更深度联动\n'@ | gh pr edit 13 --body-file -",
  "workdir": "d:\\demo1\\openmnemo",
  "timeout_ms": 100000
}

Tool Call #623 shell_command call_l8bGtxVixgc5LYWDCF5eH9eQ 10:01:25
shell_command input={"command":"@'\n## Context\nThis PR delivers AI Chat Phase 0.5 as a product-scope feature set. It introduces a usable local chat flow with persisted sessions, decoupled relay co...
Show input payload 
{
  "command": "@'\n## Context\nThis PR delivers AI Chat Phase 0.5 as a product-scope feature set. It introduces a usable local chat flow with persisted sessions, decoupled relay configuration, provider abstraction, and report-side chat rendering.\n\nThis change is intentionally separated from MemoryTree-only contract/docs updates.\n\n## Goals\n- Provide a practical local AI chat flow (not a one-off demo)\n- Support provider abstraction for OpenAI-compatible relay and Anthropic\n- Persist conversation state and preserve citation-friendly context links\n- Render chat output in report artifacts\n\n## Scope\n### Types\n- Added `packages/types/src/chat.ts`\n- Exported chat types via `packages/types/src/index.ts`\n\n### Core chat runtime\nAdded:\n- `packages/core/src/chat/chat-service.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/context-builder.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/conversation.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/llm-provider.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/local-chat-service.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/prompt.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/provider-factory.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/providers/openai-compatible.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/providers/anthropic.ts`\n- `packages/core/src/chat/index.ts`\n\nBehavior:\n- Streaming SSE and non-stream JSON relay responses are both supported\n- Relay credentials/config (`baseUrl`, `apiKey`, `model`) are decoupled from service orchestration\n- Event stream output is standardized for CLI/report reuse\n\n### CLI integration\n- Updated `packages/cli/src/cmd-report.ts`\n- Added `packages/cli/tests/cli/report-chat.test.ts`\n\n### Report integration\nAdded:\n- `packages/report/src/chat/frontend-adapter.ts`\n- `packages/report/src/chat/ui.ts`\n- `packages/report/src/render/chat.ts`\nUpdated:\n- `packages/report/src/render/layout.ts`\n- `packages/report/tests/build.test.ts`\n- `packages/report/tests/chat-frontend-adapter.test.ts`\n\n### Misc\n- Small integration touch in `packages/core/src/storage/vector/sqlite-vec-adapter.ts`\n- Lockfile refresh in `pnpm-lock.yaml`\n\n## Architecture Notes\n- Product feature line only (AI Chat Phase 0.5)\n- Not a replacement for existing data-layer interfaces\n- Not introducing SaaS/enterprise auth/multi-agent orchestration in this PR\n\n## Validation\nLocal:\n- `pnpm --filter @openmnemo/core lint` passed\n- `pnpm lint` passed\n\nCI matrix (PR #13):\n- `build-and-test (ubuntu-latest, 22)` passed\n- `build-and-test (ubuntu-latest, 24)` passed\n- `build-and-test (windows-latest, 22)` passed\n- `build-and-test (windows-latest, 24)` passed\n\n## Follow-up (Out of scope here)\n- Broader provider capability matrix and error normalization\n- Finer-grained session/citation permission policy\n- Deeper alignment with upcoming asset-layer contracts\n'@ | gh pr edit 13 --body-file -",
  "workdir": "d:\\demo1\\openmnemo",
  "timeout_ms": 100000
}

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

  "turn_id": "019d1f54-6dd9-7ab0-8a04-3ff527d1c929",

  "approval_policy": "never",

    "type": "danger-full-access"
  },
  "model": "gpt-5.4",
  "personality": "friendly",
  "collaboration_mode": {
    "mode": "default",
    "settings": {
      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

    "mode": "default",
    "settings": {
      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

  "type": "module",

    "version-packages": "changeset version",

assistant #741 final_answer 10:21:14

我认真对了 main 的代码和这 6 个 PR，结论是：确实都还没合并到 main，不是显示问题。

代码级证据（当前 main）：

- package.json 里
- @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin 还是 ^8.57.0（PR #12 目标是 8.57.1）
- typescript-eslint 还是 ^8.57.0（PR #8 目标是 8.57.1）
- eslint 还是 ^10.0.3（PR #9 目标是 10.1.0）
- turbo 还是 ^2.4.0（PR #11 目标是 2.8.20）
- packages/core/package.json / packages/cli/package.json / packages/report/package.json 里
- @anthropic-ai/sdk 还是 ^0.79.0（PR #10 目标是 0.80.0）

PR 状态也印证这一点：

- #8 MERGEABLE，CI 全绿
- #10 MERGEABLE，CI 全绿
- #9 / #11 / #12 MERGEABLE 但 CI 失败
- #1 CONFLICTING，mergeStateStatus=DIRTY，而且变更量极大（819 files / +3,057,379），不是可直接合并状态

所以你刚问“这 6 个有没有合并到 main”，答案是：没有，一个都没进 main。

    "mode": "default",
    "settings": {
      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #767 final_answer 10:28:56

你这问题很关键，我按“解决什么问题 + 属于什么实践提交”逐条说清楚：

- #1 memorytree(bootstrap): add repository memory policy
- 解决的问题：把仓库接入 MemoryTree 工作流（AGENTS.md 策略、Memory 分支流），并持续导入 transcript。
- 实践类型：MemoryTree 运维/归档实践提交（不是产品功能提交）。
- 现状：规模非常大（800+ 文件、300万+ 行），且与 main 冲突，已经不适合直接合并。

- #8 bump typescript-eslint 8.57.0 -> 8.57.1
- 解决的问题：升级 TS ESLint 工具集小版本，吃上游修复，减少 lint 规则/工具链问题。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（dev tooling）。
- 现状：可合并、CI 通过。

- #9 bump eslint 10.0.3 -> 10.1.0
- 解决的问题：升级 ESLint 主包，获得 lint 引擎的新修复。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（dev tooling）。
- 现状：可合并但 CI 当前失败（UNSTABLE）。

- #10 bump @anthropic-ai/sdk 0.79.0 -> 0.80.0
- 解决的问题：运行时 SDK 版本更新（core/cli/report 三处 package + lock）。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（runtime dependency）。
- 现状：可合并、CI 通过。

- #11 bump turbo -> 2.8.20
- 解决的问题：升级 monorepo 构建编排器 Turbo，获取任务执行稳定性修复。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（build tooling）。
- 现状：可合并但 CI 当前失败（UNSTABLE）。

- #12 bump @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin -> 8.57.1
- 解决的问题：升级 ESLint 插件包，拿到规则侧修复。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（lint tooling）。
- 现状：可合并但 CI 当前失败（UNSTABLE）。

一句话总结：
- 这 6 个里，#1 是 MemoryTree 流程沉淀/归档线；#8/#9/#10/#11/#12 都是 Dependabot 的“依赖治理实践提交”。
- 当前“可直接合”的是 #8、#10；其余需要先处理 CI 或重整分支策略。

task_completed #769 019d1f62-eb92-76f2-96c8-1d5596699856 10:28:56
你这问题很关键，我按“解决什么问题 + 属于什么实践提交”逐条说清楚： - `#1 memorytree(bootstrap): add repository memory policy` - 解决的问题：把仓库接入 MemoryTree 工作流（`AGENTS.md` 策略、Memory 分支流），并持续导入 transcript。 - 实践类型：`MemoryTree 运维/归档实践提交`（不是产品功能提交）。 - 现状：规模非常大（800+ 文件、300万+ 行）...
Show task summary

你这问题很关键，我按“解决什么问题 + 属于什么实践提交”逐条说清楚：

- #1 memorytree(bootstrap): add repository memory policy
- 解决的问题：把仓库接入 MemoryTree 工作流（AGENTS.md 策略、Memory 分支流），并持续导入 transcript。
- 实践类型：MemoryTree 运维/归档实践提交（不是产品功能提交）。
- 现状：规模非常大（800+ 文件、300万+ 行），且与 main 冲突，已经不适合直接合并。

- #8 bump typescript-eslint 8.57.0 -> 8.57.1
- 解决的问题：升级 TS ESLint 工具集小版本，吃上游修复，减少 lint 规则/工具链问题。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（dev tooling）。
- 现状：可合并、CI 通过。

- #9 bump eslint 10.0.3 -> 10.1.0
- 解决的问题：升级 ESLint 主包，获得 lint 引擎的新修复。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（dev tooling）。
- 现状：可合并但 CI 当前失败（UNSTABLE）。

- #10 bump @anthropic-ai/sdk 0.79.0 -> 0.80.0
- 解决的问题：运行时 SDK 版本更新（core/cli/report 三处 package + lock）。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（runtime dependency）。
- 现状：可合并、CI 通过。

- #11 bump turbo -> 2.8.20
- 解决的问题：升级 monorepo 构建编排器 Turbo，获取任务执行稳定性修复。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（build tooling）。
- 现状：可合并但 CI 当前失败（UNSTABLE）。

- #12 bump @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin -> 8.57.1
- 解决的问题：升级 ESLint 插件包，拿到规则侧修复。
- 实践类型：Dependabot 自动依赖治理提交（lint tooling）。
- 现状：可合并但 CI 当前失败（UNSTABLE）。

一句话总结：
- 这 6 个里，#1 是 MemoryTree 流程沉淀/归档线；#8/#9/#10/#11/#12 都是 Dependabot 的“依赖治理实践提交”。
- 当前“可直接合”的是 #8、#10；其余需要先处理 CI 或重整分支策略。

      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #789 final_answer 10:32:03

按“时间 + 风险 + 现在状态（截至 2026-03-24）”，我建议这个处理顺序：

1. 先处理 #1（不合并，直接关闭）
- PR：#1 memorytree(bootstrap)
- 原因：最老、冲突、改动量极大（800+ 文件、300万+ 行）、CONFLICTING，继续挂着只会干扰判断。
- 动作：关闭 PR；需要的 MemoryTree 内容走小而干净的新 PR（你现在已经把核心 contract 合到 main 了）。

2. 立即合并 #10（低风险、价值高）
- PR：#10 @anthropic-ai/sdk 0.79.0 -> 0.80.0
- 状态：MERGEABLE + CI 绿
- 动作：直接合并。

3. 立即合并 #8（低风险、价值中）
- PR：#8 typescript-eslint 8.57.0 -> 8.57.1
- 状态：MERGEABLE + CI 绿
- 动作：直接合并。

4. 重算后再处理 #12（中低风险）
- PR：#12 @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin 8.57.0 -> 8.57.1
- 状态：MERGEABLE 但 UNSTABLE
- 动作：在 #8/#10 合并后，先 @dependabot rebase 或 @dependabot recreate，再看 CI；绿了再合。

5. 然后处理 #9（中风险）
- PR：#9 eslint 10.0.3 -> 10.1.0
- 状态：MERGEABLE 但 CI 红
- 动作：同样先重算分支（rebase/recreate），若仍红再看具体 lint 规则变化影响。

6. 最后处理 #11（中风险偏高）
- PR：#11 turbo -> 2.8.20
- 状态：MERGEABLE 但 CI 红
- 动作：最后处理，因为会影响构建编排工具，建议在其余依赖稳定后再升级。

这套顺序的核心是：先清理噪音（#1）-> 先吃绿灯（#10/#8）-> 再解决红灯（#12/#9/#11）。
你要的话，我可以现在就按这个顺序直接替你执行（关 #1、合 #10/#8、触发其余 rebase/recreate）。

- 状态：MERGEABLE 但 CI 红
- 动作：同样先重算分支（rebase/recreate），若仍红再看具体 lint 规则变化影响。

6. 最后处理 #11（中风险偏高）
- PR：#11 turbo -> 2.8.20
- 状态：MERGEABLE 但 CI 红
- 动作：最后处理，因为会影响构建编排工具，建议在其余依赖稳定后再升级。

这套顺序的核心是：先清理噪音（#1）-> 先吃绿灯（#10/#8）-> 再解决红灯（#12/#9/#11）。
你要的话，我可以现在就按这个顺序直接替你执行（关 #1、合 #10/#8、触发其余 rebase/recreate）。

If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions.   "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #837 final_answer 10:37:58

你刚贴的这 5 个（#10/#8/#12/#9/#11）本质上都是依赖升级提交，不是功能逻辑重构。

- #10
更新 @anthropic-ai/sdk：0.79.0 -> 0.80.0
变更文件：packages/cli/package.json、packages/core/package.json、packages/report/package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
另外 packages/report/package.json 里的 peerDependencies 也同步从 ^0.79.0 改到 ^0.80.0。

- #8
更新 typescript-eslint：8.57.0 -> 8.57.1
变更文件：根 package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
主要是开发工具链小版本升级（lint 生态）。

- #12
更新 @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin：8.57.0 -> 8.57.1
变更文件：根 package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
也是 lint 插件层的补丁升级。

- #9
更新 eslint：10.0.3 -> 10.1.0
变更文件：根 package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
是 ESLint 主包升级，可能引入规则/行为变化（所以 CI 红要重点看）。

- #11
更新 turbo：2.4.0 -> 2.8.20
变更文件：根 package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
是构建编排器升级，影响构建/任务执行链路。

如果你说的是“6 个都算上”，那第 6 个 #1 不是单纯依赖升级，而是 MemoryTree bootstrap + 大量 transcript 导入。

total 12386831in 12317956out 68875reason 41882

你刚贴的这 5 个（#10/#8/#12/#9/#11）本质上都是依赖升级提交，不是功能逻辑重构。

- #10
更新 @anthropic-ai/sdk：0.79.0 -> 0.80.0
变更文件：packages/cli/package.json、packages/core/package.json、packages/report/package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
另外 packages/report/package.json 里的 peerDependencies 也同步从 ^0.79.0 改到 ^0.80.0。

- #8
更新 typescript-eslint：8.57.0 -> 8.57.1
变更文件：根 package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
主要是开发工具链小版本升级（lint 生态）。

- #12
更新 @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin：8.57.0 -> 8.57.1
变更文件：根 package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
也是 lint 插件层的补丁升级。

- #9
更新 eslint：10.0.3 -> 10.1.0
变更文件：根 package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
是 ESLint 主包升级，可能引入规则/行为变化（所以 CI 红要重点看）。

- #11
更新 turbo：2.4.0 -> 2.8.20
变更文件：根 package.json、pnpm-lock.yaml
是构建编排器升级，影响构建/任务执行链路。

如果你说的是“6 个都算上”，那第 6 个 #1 不是单纯依赖升级，而是 MemoryTree bootstrap + 大量 transcript 导入。

{
  "turn_id": "019d1f6e-e67b-79e3-88a6-08f4ca91516d",

  "current_date": "2026-03-24",
  "timezone": "Asia/Shanghai",
  "approval_policy": "never",
  "sandbox_policy": {
    "type": "danger-full-access"
  },
  "model": "gpt-5.4",
  "personality": "friendly",
  "collaboration_mode": {
    "mode": "default",
    "settings": {
      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #845 final_answer 10:41:09

你说得对，我刚才讲复杂了。直接说人话：

这 6 个里，只有两种来源。

1. Dependabot 机器人自动提的（依赖升级）
- #8、#9、#10、#11、#12
- 本质：只改版本号和 pnpm-lock.yaml
- 不是你手动写功能代码

2. MemoryTree 工作流分支提的（仓库记忆/转录导入）
- #1
- 本质：AGENTS.md + 大量 transcript/import 产物
- 不是产品功能 PR，而且体量太大、现在还冲突

所以你可以把它理解成：

- #8~#12 = 自动依赖更新 PR
- #1 = MemoryTree 历史归档 PR

如果你同意，我下一步就按这个标准继续处理：先合 #10 和 #8，再处理 #12/#9/#11。

  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",

    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}
